claim_boundary: Exact-row, same-host, bounded throughput snapshot for Llama 3.2 3B Instruct Q8_0 on one Apple M4 (16GB) host. Camelid vs llama.cpp prefill is parity-level within observed inter-round variance; MLX-LM (8-bit) prompt processing is faster than both. Not a model-family, context-sweep, or production-load claim.

host.thermal_note: warm host; rounds alternate runtime order to bound order/thermal bias

workload.model_row: Llama 3.2 3B Instruct Q8_0 (GGUF for camelid and llama.cpp; mlx-community Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct-8bit for MLX-LM)
workload.prompt: 601-token repeated-sentence prompt ('The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.' x60)
workload.camelid_command: camelid bench-generate <model> --prompt-file <601-token prompt> --max-tokens 16 --warmup --iterations 3-5 (default CLI fast stack)
workload.mlx_command: mlx_lm.generate two-point decode + 601-token verbose prompt pass (mlx-lm 0.31.3)

method: Three same-session rounds with alternating runtime order (camelid/llama.cpp/MLX; llama.cpp first in round 2, camelid first in rounds 1 and 3). Per-runtime result is the median across rounds; per-round detail retained in raw logs in this bundle.
notes: Inter-round variance for prefill is roughly +/-4% for both camelid and llama.cpp on this warm host; single-round comparisons inside that band are not decisive.
Camelid decode in this bundle (25.3 tok/s at 128 tokens) reads below both comparators; decode-path tuning is tracked separately from this prefill-focused snapshot.
MLX-LM runs its own 8-bit quantization format; prefill/decode are comparable workloads but not byte-identical weight formats.
